commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a short `docs/concepts/dreaming.md` section saying built-in dreaming is memory-core scoped and directing LanceDB users to ClawHub for LanceDB-backed dreaming plugins.

PR surface: Docs +6. Total +6 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a runtime bug, but the docs mismatch is source-reproducible: current main and `v2026.5.27` read `dreaming` config from `memory-lancedb` when it owns `plugins.slots.memory`.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow docs repair can resolve the source-backed wording mismatch without product or security design work.

target.title: docs: note built-in dreaming is memory-core scoped; LanceDB users check ClawHub
target.body:
## Summary

Documents that built-in dreaming (Light/REM/Deep) is scoped to **memory-core** and does not operate on LanceDB-backed memory stores. For LanceDB users, points to [ClawHub](https://clawhub.ai/) for community dreaming plugins.

Updated per review — generic caveat only, no package-specific install, hooks, or release links.

**Changes:** +6 lines to `docs/concepts/dreaming.md`

Closes #87535

---

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Docs-only PR — adds a "LanceDB and dreaming" subsection noting that built-in dreaming only works with memory-core, and points LanceDB users to ClawHub for community dreaming plugins.

**Environment:** OpenClaw 2026.5.27, LanceDB Pro (1000+ vectors, bge-m3 @ 1024d), Ubuntu 24.04 on Tencent Cloud Lighthouse.

**Steps to reproduce:**
PR is docs-only — no reproduction needed.

**Observed result after fix:**
Documentation change only — the added text accurately states that built-in dreaming is scoped to memory-core and points LanceDB users to ClawHub for community dreaming plugins. The community plugin `memory-lancedb-dreaming` v0.2.3 is verified running in production on the same environment (plugin logs: cronHook=ready, modelOverride=ready; dreaming pipeline produces light/rem/deep output).

**What was not tested:**
Server-side docs rendering on openclaw.ai (markdown only — maintainer can verify).
